summary: The user set up a new 55-gallon aquarium in their living room, adding Java moss and anacharis while seeking advice on plant care and algae prevention. They transferred fish from a 20-gallon tank, including angelfish and neon tetras, and are considering adding more plants and fish while ensuring the tank remains well-stocked but not overstocked.
facts: The user set up a new 55-gallon aquarium in their living room. The user added Java moss and anacharis to their aquarium. The user performs 10-15% water changes every week in their aquarium. The user transferred all their fish from a 20-gallon tank to the new 55-gallon tank. The user has a pair of angelfish and a school of neon tetras in their 55-gallon tank. The user lost a few neon tetras in the first week after introducing them to the tank. The user has been feeding their fish a mix of flakes, pellets, and frozen brine shrimp. The user is considering adding more Java moss or anacharis to the tank. The user is thinking of adding dwarf gouramis or harlequin rasboras to the tank. The user is researching to determine the best additions to avoid overstocking the tank.
